seo_title: Optimizing Rollout Allocation for Group-Based RL Post-Training
meta_description: A budget-aware rollout allocation framework for group-based RL post-training, improving accuracy and reducing sampling costs.
body_html:
Pilot-Commit improves accuracy and reduces sampling costs for group-based RL post-training by decoupling prompt evaluation from exploitation.

Key Benefits of Pilot-CommitImproves accuracy up to 1.9 imes faster than GRPO and 4.0 imes faster than DAPO in cumulative rollouts.Reduces sampling costs by allocating budget to high-leverage prompts while skipping low-signal prompts.Pilot-Commit ArchitectureComponentDescriptionPilot StageEvaluates per-prompt informativeness using a fraction of the budget.Exploitation StageAllocates remaining rollouts to high-leverage prompts while skipping low-signal prompts.Comparison with Baseline MethodsPilot-Commit outperforms baseline methods in multiple math reasoning benchmarks and model scales, demonstrating its effectiveness in improving accuracy and reducing sampling costs.

source.title: Spend Your Rollouts Where It Counts: Rollout Allocation for Group-Based RL Post-Training
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Pilot-Commit to optimize the allocation of expensive rollouts in reinforcement learning (RL) post-training for large language models. The current methods waste resources on prompts with negligible learning signals, but Pilot-Commit allocates budget more efficiently by estimating per-prompt informativeness and prioritizing high-leverage prompts. This approach has been shown to match baseline accuracy while reducing sampling costs, outperforming existing methods in multiple benchmarks and model scales.
